topic.title: HTML Link Actions and Safety
topic.description: HTML links trigger email, calls, and file downloads and need safety attributes for new-tab targets.

content.text: Link Actions, Downloads, and Security Attributes

text.value: element does more than navigate to pages. The
dials a phone number on mobile devices, and
attribute instructs the browser to save the linked file rather than navigate to it. Opening links in a new tab with
to prevent the opened page from accessing
and reading or redirecting the originating page. Together these attributes cover the full range of link behaviours required in real HTML documents. Reference:

content.title: target='_blank' Without rel='noopener' Is a Security Risk
content.text: When a page opens a link with target='_blank', the opened page gets a reference to the opener via window.opener. A malicious page can use this to redirect the original tab to a phishing URL while the user is reading the new tab. Adding rel='noopener noreferrer' severs this connection. Modern browsers apply noopener automatically for cross-origin blank links, but explicit rel attributes remain best practice for all blank-target links.

examples.title: Email, Phone, and SMS Links with Pre-filled Parameters
examples.description: This example shows every special scheme link: a plain mailto link, a mailto with subject and body pre-filled using URL encoding, a telephone link with international format, an SMS link, and a combined contact card using all three. It also shows the accessible pattern for links that open non-web resources.
examples.code_snippet:
Email, Phone, and SMS Link Actions

1. Basic mailto link

hello@devdocs.in

2. mailto with pre-filled subject and body

Send course enquiry

Raw href value for reference:

mailto:hello@devdocs.in?subject=Course%20Enquiry&body=Hi%20team...

3. tel: phone link

+91 98765 43210

4. sms: text message link

Send SMS
Send pre-filled SMS

5. Contact card with all three link types

DevDocs India Support

Available Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 6 PM IST

Email support Call us Send SMS

6. Accessible labels for action links

+91 98765 43210

Call our Kozhikode office: +91 495 234 5678
examples.output: Page renders six sections. The basic mailto shows a blue email link. The pre-filled mailto link opens a mail client with subject 'Course Enquiry' and a multi-line body. The tel link shows a formatted Indian phone number. The sms section shows both a plain SMS link and one with a pre-filled body. The contact card renders three coloured buttons for email, call, and SMS with inline SVG icons. The accessibility section shows the correct and incorrect labelling patterns for action links.

examples.title: Download Links, New-Tab Safety, and rel Attributes
examples.description: This example shows the download attribute for file links with custom filenames, the safe target='_blank' pattern with rel='noopener noreferrer', the noreferrer effect on the Referer header, and a comparison of safe versus unsafe blank links. It also demonstrates rel='external' and rel='sponsored' for content classification.
examples.code_snippet:
Download Links and Secure External Links

1. The download attribute

PDF
HTML Cheatsheet
PDF · 2.4 MB · Updated March 2025
ZIP
Course Assets
ZIP · 14.7 MB · Starter files for all modules
CSV
Student Data Export
CSV · 48 KB · Comma-separated values

2. target="_blank" — unsafe vs safe

Unsafe — window.opener exposed

<a href="https://example.com" target="_blank">Example</a>

Safe — window.opener severed

<a href="https://example.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Example</a>

MDN anchor element (opens in new tab, safe)

3. rel attribute values for links

rel value Effect Use when
noopener Sets window.opener to null All target=_blank links
noreferrer Suppresses Referer header + implies noopener External links where referrer privacy matters
external Semantic: link goes outside the site Any cross-domain link for SEO clarity
nofollow Tells search engines not to follow User-generated content, paid links
sponsored Declares a paid or sponsored link Affiliate or advertising links
ugc Marks user-generated content links Comments, forum posts, reviews

4. Combining download with target="_blank"

Download (same tab, browser saves file)

<a href="/report.pdf" download>Download report</a>

New tab (opens PDF in browser viewer)

<a href="/report.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">View report</a>
examples.output: Page renders four sections. The download section shows three styled file cards for PDF, ZIP, and CSV with file icons, sizes, and download arrows; clicking each triggers a browser save dialog with the custom filename. The unsafe vs safe section shows red and green code cards illustrating the vulnerable and secure blank-target patterns, followed by a live MDN link that opens safely in a new tab. The rel reference table covers noopener, noreferrer, external, nofollow, sponsored, and ugc values. The final section clarifies that download and target='_blank' are mutually exclusive.

attribute with a descriptive filename value for all file links so users receive a clearly named file rather than a URL-derived name.

attribute gives developers control over how the browser handles file links, preventing unwanted in-browser navigation to PDFs and other viewable files.

attribute is a universal resource locator, not just a web page address. The scheme prefix determines what the browser does with it.
embeds the content inline. The browser delegates to the operating system for any scheme it does not handle natively.
When a page opens a new tab with
, the browser creates a new browsing context. Without
reference. The new tab can set
to any URL, replacing the original page behind the user's back.
nullifies this reference at the moment the new context is created.
value serves two purposes: it implies
(security) and it instructs the browser to omit the
request header (privacy). Without it, the destination server sees the full URL of the page the user clicked from.
is appropriate for links to external sites where revealing the originating URL would expose internal URLs or user session tokens in query strings.
lets the opened page redirect the original tab.
links causes them to fail on devices in different regions.
query values creates malformed links that open an empty email draft.
to download links to announce the file type and size to screen reader users:
. Without it, screen readers read only the visible link text, giving no indication that the link triggers a download rather than navigation.
or JavaScript check to show the call button only on touch devices. On desktop, a click-to-call link opens video conferencing apps unexpectedly. CSS
targets touch-primary devices and can be used to show or hide the telephone link without any JavaScript.

subtopics.title: Email and Telephone Links
subtopics.description: mailto and tel URL schemes open the email client or phone dialler directly from a link click.

text.value: opens the user's default email client with the address pre-filled. Optional query parameters
pre-populate those fields. All parameter values must be
scheme opens the phone dialler on mobile and a VOIP app on desktop. Always use
links to ensure correct dialling internationally. Reference:

examples.title: mailto and tel Links with All Parameters
examples.description: This example shows every mailto parameter combination, the correct URL-encoding for multi-line body text, the international tel format, and the sms scheme. It also demonstrates the accessible labelling approach when the link text is not the phone number itself.
examples.code_snippet:
mailto and tel Link Reference

1. Plain mailto

href="mailto:hello@devdocs.in"

hello@devdocs.in

2. mailto with subject only

href="mailto:hello@devdocs.in?subject=Course%20Enquiry"

Send course enquiry

3. mailto with subject and multi-line body

href="mailto:hello@devdocs.in
  ?subject=HTML%20Course%20Enquiry
  &body=Hi%20team%2C%0A%0A...
  &cc=courses@devdocs.in"

Send detailed enquiry (pre-filled)

4. tel link — E.164 international format

href="tel:+919876543210"

+91 98765 43210

Kozhikode office: +91 495 234 5678

5. sms link

href="sms:+919876543210"

Send us an SMS

SMS with pre-filled message

6. Common encoding mistakes

Wrong: unencoded spaces break the mailto

href="mailto:x@x.com?subject=Hello World"

Right: spaces as %20

href="mailto:x@x.com?subject=Hello%20World"

Wrong: local tel without country code

href="tel:09876543210" — fails outside India

Right: E.164 with country code

href="tel:+919876543210"
examples.output: Page renders six annotated sections. The plain mailto shows an email address as a link. The subject-only mailto opens a compose window with the subject pre-filled. The full mailto with subject, multi-line body, and CC parameter opens a compose window with all fields populated, the multi-line body using %0A for newlines. The tel section shows a formatted display number linking to the E.164 href, and a context-labelled office number. The sms section shows plain and pre-filled message links. The encoding mistakes section shows the wrong versus right patterns for spaces in subject and local versus international tel format.

text.value: link is not a form submission. The browser hands the entire

URI to the operating system, which passes it to the registered email application. The pre-filled subject and body are suggestions, not guarantees: users can edit or clear them before sending. Each email client parses the URI slightly differently, so complex body formatting with special characters should be tested across Mail, Outlook, and Gmail.

scheme was designed for PSTN phone numbers but modern devices route it to whatever app is registered for calling: Phone, FaceTime, Google Voice, WhatsApp, or Skype. On desktop it typically opens the last-registered VOIP app, which may surprise users expecting a phone call. Showing the number in visible text alongside the link gives users enough context to decide whether to click.
on every parameter value rather than hand-encoding strings.
automatically, handling all special characters including ampersands, quotes, and newlines that are easy to miss when encoding by hand.

subtopics.title: Downloadable File Links
subtopics.description: The download attribute instructs the browser to save a linked file instead of navigating to it.

text.value: element changes the browser's behaviour from navigating to the linked URL to saving the resource as a file. The attribute can be used as a bare boolean (
) to keep the server filename, or given a string value (
URLs. For cross-origin URLs the browser ignores
and navigates instead. The server can also force a download for any URL by sending
in the response header, regardless of whether
is present on the link. Reference:

examples.title: Download Attribute with Custom Filenames and Blob URLs
examples.description: This example shows the download attribute without a value (keep server filename), with a custom filename string, a download link pointing to a blob URL generated by JavaScript, and the server Content-Disposition header as a server-side alternative. It also shows the accessibility pattern for download links.
examples.code_snippet:
Downloadable File Links

1. download with no value (keep server filename)

Download HTML guide

2. download with custom filename

Download HTML Guide (PDF, 2.4 MB)

3. Styled download button with accessible label

Download Course Assets

4. Blob URL download (JavaScript-generated file)

5. Server-side alternative: Content-Disposition header


Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="DevDocs-HTML-Guide-2025.pdf"
Content-Type: application/pdf

6. Cross-origin download limitation


<a href="https://cdn.external.com/file.pdf" download>
  Download (won't work — browser navigates instead)
</a>


<!-- OR set Content-Disposition: attachment on the CDN response -->
examples.output: Page renders six sections. The bare download link saves with the URL filename. The custom filename link triggers a save dialog with the specified name. The styled download button shows a blue pill button with a download arrow SVG and an accessible aria-label. The blob URL section shows a green 'Generate and download CSV' button that creates a CSV file from JavaScript data and triggers an immediate download. The server-side section shows the Content-Disposition header syntax. The cross-origin section shows the limitation code block with the correct alternative patterns.

text.value: attribute is a client-side instruction to the browser. It changes the browser's intent from
save a file from this URL
. However, the browser can only honour this instruction for resources it has
to. For cross-origin resources the server controls download behaviour through the
response header, which works regardless of origin.
URL is a temporary local reference to an in-memory object. The pattern of creating a blob, making a temporary
, programmatically clicking it, and then calling
is the standard way to trigger a file download from JavaScript-generated content without any server interaction. The
call is essential: without it the blob object stays in memory for the lifetime of the page.
to describe the file type and size on download links:

. Screen readers announce this before the user activates the link, so they can decide whether to wait for a large download. Without it, screen reader users hear only the visible link text with no indication that the link triggers a save dialog.

subtopics.title: Secure Link Attributes (noopener, noreferrer)
subtopics.description: noopener severs window.opener on blank links and noreferrer also removes the Referer request header.

text.value: link opens a new browsing context, the opened page receives a
reference pointing back to the originating page. A malicious site can exploit this to redirect the opener with
HTTP request header. Modern browsers apply
, but explicit attributes remain required for same-origin blank links and for older browser support. Reference:

examples.title: Tab-napping Demo and Safe External Link Patterns
examples.description: This example shows the window.opener vulnerability with a code walkthrough, the correct rel attribute combination for all blank-target link scenarios, how noreferrer affects the Referer header, and a practical component that opens external documentation safely.
examples.code_snippet:
Secure Link Attributes: noopener and noreferrer

1. How tab-napping works (without noopener)


<a href="https://malicious-site.example.com" target="_blank">
  Read this article
</a>


if (window.opener) {
  // Redirect the original tab to a phishing page
  // while the user is reading this new tab
  window.opener.location = 'https://your-site-phishing-copy.com/login';
}
// User switches back to original tab and sees a fake login page
// They may enter credentials, thinking they were logged out

2. The safe pattern

Unsafe — window.opener accessible

<a href="https://ext.com" target="_blank">Read more</a>

Safe — noopener + noreferrer

<a href="https://ext.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Read more</a>

3. Effect of noreferrer on the HTTP Referer header


GET /article HTTP/1.1
Host: ext.com
Referer: https://devdocs.in/html/forms?token=abc123&session=xyz


GET /article HTTP/1.1
Host: ext.com

Use noreferrer when your URLs contain session tokens, user IDs, or sensitive query parameters that would be exposed to the destination site via the Referer header.

4. Choosing the right rel value


<a href="https://github.com/user/repo"
   target="_blank"
   rel="noopener noreferrer">GitHub repo</a>


<a href="https://forum.example.com"
   target="_blank"
   rel="noreferrer">Forum discussion</a>


<a href="/docs/api"
   target="_blank"
   rel="noopener">Open API docs in new tab</a>


<a href="https://sponsor.com"
   rel="noopener noreferrer sponsored nofollow">
  Sponsored link
</a>

5. External resource links (all safe)

  • MDN HTML Reference [opens new tab]
  • WHATWG HTML Living Standard [opens new tab]
  • WCAG 2.1 Quick Reference [opens new tab]
examples.output: Page renders five sections. The tab-napping section shows a code walkthrough of how window.opener is exploited in a new tab. The safe pattern section shows red and green code cards for unsafe and safe blank links. The Referer section shows HTTP request headers with and without noreferrer, illustrating how session tokens in query strings are exposed without it. The choosing section shows four code examples for the different link scenarios. The external resource list shows three live external links all using the safe rel combination.

text.value: reference is a relic of a web model where related tabs were expected to communicate freely.

reflects a modern security principle: browsing contexts should be isolated by default. The new tab gets no reference back to the page that spawned it, just as a new browser window opened by typing a URL directly has no

header (note: misspelled in the HTTP specification, historically) tells the destination server which page the user came from. This is useful for analytics but leaks information when the originating URL contains query parameters like
omits this header entirely. The newer
HTTP response header gives servers finer control over referrer behaviour across all links on a page.
to apply a referrer policy page-wide instead of repeating
on every individual link. The value
sends the origin only (no path or query) to cross-origin destinations, which is a good default for most sites.

seo_metadata.meta_title: HTML Link Actions and Safety | mailto, tel, download, noopener Guide
seo_metadata.meta_description: Master HTML link actions: mailto and tel URL schemes, the download attribute, and rel noopener noreferrer for safe target='_blank' external links.
